quotes.verbatim_quote: Here's the same phrase in the 7th position. This changes the picking considerably.
quotes.why_load_bearing: States Bruno's core principle that fingerboard position dictates picking pattern, so the same melodic phrase requires re-thought stroke direction when relocated.
Names the technique category that closes the chapter and links be-bop phrasing to slur articulation rather than pure alternate picking.

quotes.verbatim_quote: I prefer the 2nd example; it sounds more horn-like
quotes.why_load_bearing: Bruno's explicit aesthetic rule: slurred triplets win over picked ones for be-bop because they imitate saxophone phrasing, a load-bearing stylistic prescription.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Learn how to slur triplets with a "down-stroke" and an "up-stroke"
quotes.why_load_bearing: Prescribes that the slurred-triplet technique must be mastered starting from either pick direction, defining the drill that exercise 35 enforces.
Frames the symmetry requirement: any be-bop slurred figure must also be playable beginning with an up-stroke, reinforcing the chapter's two-direction discipline.
